summary: Today's sweep leans toward Democracy / press freedom, AI / computing, Games / criticism. The strongest items in this run are D4vd arrested on suspicion of killing teen girl whose body was found in his Tesla, Acting ICE director Todd Lyons will step down at the end of May, says DHS, and OpenAI starts offering a biology-tuned LLM.

mainItems.title: Acting ICE director Todd Lyons will step down at the end of May, says DHS
mainItems.summary: Lyons, who led agency since March 2025, to resign after turbulent year carrying out Trump’s immigration agenda Todd Lyons, the acting director of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), is stepping down after a turbulent year…

mainItems.title: Now you can break up with big tech at a bar: ‘cybersecurity disguised as a party’
mainItems.summary: These digital security organizers bring the fight for online privacy to dance parties, wine meetups and reading groups Imani Thompson shows up at Wonderville Bar in Brooklyn looking ready for a DJ set, or to drink, or to dance the night…

mainItems.summary: The adaptation of the Capcom game looks fun, silly The post The Street Fighter Trailer Looks Amazing, and We’re as Surprised as You Are appeared first on Reactor .

mainItems.title: Trump’s triumphal arch moves closer to approval after key agency meets
mainItems.summary: US Commission of Fine Arts reviews plan while judge lets below-ground work at White House ballroom site proceed Donald Trump’s design for the triumphal arch he wants built at an entrance to the nation’s capital moved a step forward on…

mainItems.title: Opinion: Rebuilding the Black Teacher Pipeline, for the Benefit of All Students
mainItems.summary: Across Pennsylvania, districts are struggling to recruit, prepare and retain Black teachers, who make up just 3.7% of the commonwealth’s educator workforce.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, student agency, and school policy.

mainItems.title: Parents, Schools Clash Over Movement to Abolish Screens
mainItems.summary: With more parents pushing for limits on screen time in the classroom, Vermont state Rep. Rob Hunter, a Democrat, wants to make it easier for them to opt their children out of using laptops and iPads.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Directly relevant to teaching, classroom practice, and educational judgment.

mainItems.title: App Stores Push Users Toward Nudify Apps, New Research Shows
mainItems.summary: Findings from the Tech Transparency Project claim that Google and Apple’s app stores not only host harmful apps that can undress images of women, but encourage users to find them.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Relevant to your current interest graph.

mainItems.title: Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief
mainItems.summary: The nomination comes after months of interim leadership at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

mainItems.title: The secret to mixing macarons: Macaronage is key.
mainItems.summary: For all their simplicity, macarons can be difficult to make at home. Their light-as-air texture, crisp outer shell, and signature foot rely on good technique. In short, it comes down to how you mix the macaron batter.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Lower-stakes, but high fit for the maker/craft side of your brain.

mainItems.title: A Polymarket trader made $300,000 betting on Biden's pardons, a new analysis shows
mainItems.summary: In the final hours of President Biden's term, an anonymous prediction market trader placed lucrative bets on who would be pardoned even as the odds were nearly zero.
mainItems.whyItMatters: Interesting where engineering, science, and tech infrastructure collide.

new.title: Scavengers Reign
new.why: Animated speculative sci-fi with strong worldbuilding and a weirder imagination than most prestige SF.

new.title: Sugar
new.why: Noir detective structure with a genre wrinkle and a sensibility that feels adjacent to your crime/strangeness overlap.

new.title: The Lazarus Project
new.why: Time-loop espionage, moral pressure, and speculative plotting — very plausible fit.

new.title: Bodies
new.why: Time-spanning conspiracy/mystery structure that fits your taste for long-arc puzzle storytelling.

older.title: Counterpart
older.why: Probably the single cleanest “James show you may have missed”: espionage + parallel-world sci-fi + adult competence.

older.title: Patriot
older.why: Dry, strange, melancholy spy storytelling with a very distinct voice.

older.title: Utopia
older.why: Paranoid conspiracy energy, formal boldness, and a willingness to get strange and ugly.

older.title: Rubicon
older.why: A low-key conspiracy thriller that leans heavily on intelligence work, paranoia, and institutional atmosphere.

shared.title: Bad Sisters
shared.why: Darkly funny, well-observed, and ensemble-driven in a way that plausibly overlaps with Hacks / Derry Girls / Mare of Easttown energy.

shared.title: Detectorists
shared.why: Gentle, funny, humane, and specific — a softer shared-watch possibility.

shared.title: Astrid et Raphaëlle
shared.why: French procedural with a strong central duo and exactly the kind of foreign-crime appeal Maggie may share with you.

shared.title: Somebody Somewhere
shared.why: A warmer character-driven recommendation if the shared overlap leans more Bear / Good Place than pure detective work.

highlights.title: A.I. Is Prompting an Evolution, Not Extinction, for Coders
highlights.notes: Interesting piece on the current state of AI in software work.

highlights.title: The Workers Letting A.I. Do Their Jobs
highlights.notes: In an era of agents powered by artificial intelligence, many programmers are barely programming.
